heading: Establishment, composition, and meetings
label: 6921 Establishment, composition, and meetings
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)6921(a)40:821 (related to creation)., §§ 201 (related to creation), 202(a), , .Pub. L. 90–264, title II82 Stat. 45Mar. 12, 19686921(b)(1)40:822(a) (1st sentence).6921(b)(2)40:822(a) (3d sentence).6921(b)(3)40:822(a) (2d sentence).6921(c)40:822(a) (last sentence).

Termination of Advisory CommissionsAdvisory commissions established after , to terminate not later than the expiration of the 2-year period beginning on the date of their establishment, unless, in the case of a commission established by the President or an officer of the Federal Government, such commission is renewed by appropriate action prior to the expiration of such 2-year period, or in the case of a commission established by the Congress, its duration is otherwise provided by law. See sections 1001(2) and 1013 of Title 5, Government Organization and Employees.Jan. 5, 1973

children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: a .—Establishment
children.text: There is a National Visitor Facilities Advisory Commission.

children.label: C
children.text: the Secretary of the Smithsonian Institution;

children.label: D
children.text: the Chairman of the National Capital Planning Commission;

children.label: E
children.text: the Chairman of the Commission of Fine Arts;

children.label: F
children.text: six Members of the Senate, three from each party, to be appointed by the President of the Senate;

children.label: G
children.text: six Members of the House of Representatives, three from each party, to be appointed by the Speaker of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: H
children.text: three individuals appointed by the President, at least two of whom shall not be officers of the Federal Government, and one member of whom shall be a representative of the District of Columbia government.

children.heading: .—Chairman
children.label: 2 .—Chairman
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior serves as the Chairman of the Commission.

children.heading: .—Service of non-federal members
children.label: 3 .—Service of non-federal members
children.text: Non-federal members serve at the pleasure of the President.

children.heading: .—Meetings
children.label: c .—Meetings
children.text: The Commission shall meet at the call of the Chairman.
